content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Handled explicit-id aliases when validating and verifying mapped_to_bead_id. The prompt builder canonicalizes bead IDs to the qualified form (milestone_id.short_id, e.g. '9ni.8.5.3'), but explicit beads may exist in .beads/ under their authored short ID (e.g. '8.5.3'). Three coordinated changes resolve both forms: (1) extract_pe_bead_ids in task_prompt_contract.rs now includes both the canonical form and the short-form alias (strip milestone prefix up to first dot) for each bullet item, so reviewers can reference either form and pass validation. Added a public strip_milestone_prefix helper for reuse. (2) verify_bead_exists in planned_elsewhere.rs was split into a top-level function that first tries br show with the canonical ID, and on definitive 'not found', retries with the short-form alias via strip_milestone_prefix. The try_br_show helper handles a single br show attempt. BeadVerification::Verified now carries the resolved bead ID (the form that succeeded), and MappingVerificationOutcome gains a resolved_bead_id field. (3) Both comment-posting paths — reconcile_planned_elsewhere (direct handler) and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success Phase 3 (success_reconciliation.rs) — now use the resolved bead ID for br comments add, ensuring comments target the form that br show verified.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added strip_milestone_prefix() public helper in task_prompt_contract.rs that strips the milestone-id prefix (everything up to and including the first dot) from a canonical bead ID, returning the short form or None if no dot is present.
2. [Completed] Updated extract_pe_bead_ids in task_prompt_contract.rs to insert the short-form alias (via strip_milestone_prefix) for each canonical bead ID extracted from bullet lines, so reviewers can reference either the qualified or short form.
3. [Completed] Changed BeadVerification::Verified from a unit variant to Verified(String) carrying the resolved bead ID that br show succeeded with. Added resolved_bead_id: Option<String> field to MappingVerificationOutcome. Updated all three match sites (reconcile_planned_elsewhere, verify_mappings) to destructure and propagate the resolved ID.
4. [Completed] Split verify_bead_exists into a top-level function + try_br_show helper. The top-level function tries the canonical ID first; on definitive Stale, retries with the short-form alias from strip_milestone_prefix. If the alias succeeds, returns Verified with the alias ID.
5. [Completed] Updated comment posting in reconcile_planned_elsewhere (planned_elsewhere.rs) to use the resolved bead ID (falling back to the original mapped_to_bead_id) as the br comments add target.
6. [Completed] Updated Phase 3 comment posting in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs) to use outcome.resolved_bead_id as the br comments add target, falling back to the original mapped_to_bead_id.
7. [Completed] Added extract_pe_bead_ids_includes_short_form_aliases test confirming that '9ni.8.5.3' produces both canonical and '8.5.3' short form in the extracted set, while 'plain-id' (no dot) produces only itself.
8. [Completed] Added strip_milestone_prefix_extracts_short_id test covering dotted IDs ('9ni.8.5.3' → '8.5.3', 'ms-alpha.bead-4' → 'bead-4'), no-dot IDs (None), and trailing-dot IDs (None).
9. [Completed] Ran cargo test (691 lib + 174 integration + 1 tmux + 758 unit, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings), cargo fmt --check (clean after one auto-fix for try_br_show signature).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: all tests pass across all suites (691 lib up from 689, 174 integration, 1 tmux, 758 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo test --lib extract_pe_bead_ids_includes_short_form_aliases: 1 new test passes, confirms both '9ni.8.5.3' and '8.5.3' are in the extracted set
- cargo test --lib strip_milestone_prefix_extracts_short_id: 1 new test passes, confirms correct prefix stripping for dotted, non-dotted, and trailing-dot inputs
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (task_prompt_contract.rs, planned_elsewhere.rs, success_reconciliation.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 3 files changed, 102 insertions, 12 deletions
- Files modified: src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs (strip_milestone_prefix helper, short-form alias in extract_pe_bead_ids, 2 new tests), src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs (Verified(String), try_br_show helper, alias retry in verify_bead_exists, resolved_bead_id in outcomes, comment target resolution), src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (resolved_bead_id in Phase 3 comment target)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The short-form alias is derived by stripping everything up to and including the first dot. This assumes milestone IDs do not contain dots, which holds for all current milestone IDs (e.g. '9ni', 'test-ms', 'ms-alpha'). If a future milestone ID contains a dot, the alias stripping would produce an incorrect short form.
- The alias retry in verify_bead_exists makes one additional br show call when the canonical ID is not found. This is negligible overhead since it only triggers for 'not found' results and explicit beads are a minority of all beads.
- If both the canonical and short forms exist as separate beads (hypothetical edge case), verify_bead_exists would verify with the canonical form first and only try the short form on 'not found'. This prevents ambiguity since the canonical form takes priority.
